specification.topic-statement: The Google Gemini request serializer transforms a canonical LLM request into the Gemini API JSON format, assembling system instructions, message contents, tool definitions, tool configuration, and generation configuration, and also produces the URL and HTTP headers needed to deliver the request.

scope.in-scope: System instruction serialization, message contents serialization, tool definition serialization including parameter schema cleanup, tool configuration serialization, generation configuration serialization including thinking configuration for Gemini 2.5 and Gemini 3, URL construction for streaming and non-streaming modes, and HTTP header construction.
scope.boundary: How individual message content blocks (text, thinking, tool call, tool result) are transformed into Gemini parts format is covered by the Google content serialization specification.
How thinking levels are translated into budgets or level strings per model is covered by the Google thinking configuration specification.
How the Gemini API response is parsed back into normalized stream events is covered by the Google streaming event processing specification.

field.name: system_prompt
field.note: serialized as systemInstruction; skipped when null or empty string

field.name: messages
field.note: serialized as contents array; each message contributes one content object

field.name: model
field.note: used for series classification (Gemini 2.5 vs Gemini 3) and thinking support checks

field.name: tool_count
field.note: when zero, both tools and toolConfig sections are omitted entirely

field.name: tools
field.note: each has name, description, and parameters JSON string

field.name: max_output_tokens
field.note: when greater than zero, emitted as maxOutputTokens in generationConfig

field.name: thinking.level
field.note: MIN/LOW/MED/HIGH; drives thinking configuration inclusion and value

field.name: systemInstruction
field.note: present only when system prompt is non-null and non-empty; contains parts array with a single text part

field.name: contents
field.note: always present; one object per message with role and parts

field.name: tools
field.note: present only when tool_count > 0; wraps functionDeclarations array inside a single object

field.name: toolConfig
field.note: present only when tool_count > 0; contains functionCallingConfig with mode string

field.name: generationConfig
field.note: present only when maxOutputTokens > 0 or thinking config is needed; contains maxOutputTokens and optionally thinkingConfig

behavior.system-instruction-serialization:
When the system prompt is null or an empty string, no system instruction is added to the document and serialization continues normally.
When the system prompt is non-null and non-empty, a "systemInstruction" key is added to the root object. Its value is an object with a single "parts" key whose value is an array containing one object. That object has a single "text" key set to the system prompt string.
Both a null system prompt and a zero-length system prompt string result in the systemInstruction key being absent from the output. Callers that pass an empty string get the same output as callers that pass null.
behavior.contents-serialization:
All messages are serialized in order into a "contents" array on the root object. Each message produces one content object with a "role" string and a "parts" array. Role mapping and parts assembly are delegated to the content block serializer, which is invoked once per message.
Before iterating messages, the thought signature for Gemini 3 models is located by scanning assistant messages in reverse order. If found, the signature string is preserved for injection into the first assistant message during parts serialization.
The thought signature is resolved once before iteration begins and is passed unchanged to each message's parts serialization. Injection into the message body is determined by the content block serializer when it encounters the first assistant-role message.
The thought signature lookup is skipped entirely for non-Gemini-3 models. The signature value passed to the content serializer is null when the model is not Gemini 3 or when no assistant message with a stored signature is found.
behavior.tool-definitions-serialization:
When tool_count is zero, no "tools" key is added to the root object.
When tools are present, a "tools" array is added to the root object. The array contains a single object with a "functionDeclarations" key. The value of "functionDeclarations" is an array with one entry per tool.
Each function declaration object contains: - "name": the tool name string - "description": the tool description string - "parameters": the parsed parameter schema as a JSON object
The tool parameters are stored in canonical form as a JSON string. They are parsed into a live JSON object during serialization, then the "additionalProperties" key is removed from the top-level parameters object before embedding it in the declaration.
The removal of "additionalProperties" is applied only to the top-level parameters object, not recursively to nested schema objects. Nested schemas that contain "additionalProperties" are left unchanged.
All function declarations are collected into one object containing "functionDeclarations". The "tools" array always contains exactly one such wrapper object, regardless of how many tools are present.
behavior.tool-configuration-serialization:
When tool_count is zero, no "toolConfig" key is added to the root object.
When tools are present, a "toolConfig" object is added to the root object. It contains a "functionCallingConfig" object with a "mode" string mapped from the canonical tool choice mode:
- 0 (auto) → "AUTO" - 1 (none) → "NONE" - 2 (required/any) → "ANY" - Any other value → "AUTO" (default fallback)
Tool configuration is absent if and only if tool_count is zero. It is never absent when tools are present, even if the mode is the default AUTO value.
behavior.generation-config-serialization:
A "generationConfig" object is added to the root object only when at least one of the following is true: - max_output_tokens is greater than zero - Thinking configuration must be included
If neither condition holds, the generationConfig key is absent entirely.
When present, the generationConfig object contains:
When max_output_tokens is greater than zero, a "maxOutputTokens" integer field is added with that value. When max_output_tokens is zero or absent, the field is omitted.
Whether thinking configuration is included depends on model series:
For Gemini 3 models: thinkingConfig is always included, regardless of thinking level, because Gemini 3 has no concept of "no thinking" — the lowest level is still an active thinking mode.
For Gemini 2.5 models: thinkingConfig is included only when the thinking level is not MIN and the model supports thinking. At MIN level, no thinkingConfig is emitted (unless Gemini 3 logic applies).
For all other models: thinkingConfig is never included.
When thinkingConfig is included, the object always contains: - "includeThoughts": true (boolean, always present when thinkingConfig is emitted)
Additionally: - For Gemini 2.5: a "thinkingBudget" integer field is added with the computed token budget for the given model and level. The budget value is omitted if the computed budget is negative (indicating the model is not in the table), though in practice Gemini 2.5 models in the table always return a non-negative budget. - For Gemini 3: a "thinkingLevel" string field is added with the level string for the given model and level.
Even when the user selects the lowest thinking level on a Gemini 3 model, thinkingConfig with "includeThoughts": true and a thinkingLevel string is always emitted. The Gemini 3 API does not support omitting the thinking config entirely.
The "includeThoughts" field is set to true unconditionally whenever thinkingConfig is included. There is no path that emits thinkingConfig with "includeThoughts": false.
behavior.serialization-order:
The five components are assembled in a fixed order onto the root object: 1. systemInstruction 2. contents 3. tools 4. toolConfig 5. generationConfig
Each component is serialized and added independently. Failure in any component aborts serialization and returns a parse error result. On failure the partially built document is discarded.
behavior.url-construction:
The endpoint URL is constructed from four inputs: base URL, model identifier, API key, and streaming flag.
The URL format is:
{base_url}/models/{model}:{method}?key={api_key}{alt_param}
Where: - method is "streamGenerateContent" when streaming, "generateContent" when non-streaming - alt_param is "&alt=sse" when streaming, empty string when non-streaming
The streaming alt parameter is appended after the API key query parameter with an ampersand separator, not as a separate query parameter with a question mark.
behavior.header-construction:
HTTP headers are constructed based on the streaming flag.
For streaming requests, two headers are produced: - "Content-Type: application/json" - "Accept: text/event-stream"
For non-streaming requests, one header is produced: - "Content-Type: application/json"
The header array is null-terminated in both cases. The array length (excluding the null terminator) is 2 for streaming and 1 for non-streaming.
